title: A segmentation fault occurs in the case when using 'stat' command for loaded and checked by 'equiv_opt' design.
body:
It occurs only for coolrunner2 architecture.

## Steps to reproduce the issue

**Test case:**
module dff
    ( input d, clk, output reg q );
    initial begin
      q = 0;
    end
	always @( posedge clk )
            q <= d;
endmodule

module dffe
    ( input d, clk, en, output reg q );
    initial begin
      q = 0;
    end
	always @( posedge clk)
		if ( en )
			q <= d;
endmodule

**script:**
read_verilog ../top.v
design -save read

hierarchy -top dff
proc
equiv_opt -map +/coolrunner2/cells_sim.v synth_coolrunner2 # equivalency check
design -load postopt # load the post-opt design (otherwise equiv_opt loads the pre-opt design)
cd dff # Constrain all select calls below inside the top module
stat

design -load read
hierarchy -top dffe
proc
equiv_opt -map +/coolrunner2/cells_sim.v synth_coolrunner2 # equivalency check
design -load postopt # load the post-opt design (otherwise equiv_opt loads the pre-opt design)
cd dffe # Constrain all select calls below inside the top module
stat

## Expected behavior

Print statistics

## Actual behavior

....
Found a total of 1 unproven $equiv cells.

yosys> design -load postopt

yosys> cd dff

yosys [dff]> stat

Segmentation fault (core dumped)
